Title: Quality listings key to market attractiveness: SC chairman
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia needs to improve the quality of listed companies, strengthen corporate governance and encourage greater involvement by institutional investors to make its capital market more attractive to investors.
Securities Commission (SC) chairman Datuk Mohammad Faiz Azmi said these measures are key to enhancing the market's investability.
Good corporate governance alone was insufficient to attract investors, he said, stressing that liquidity, free float and better price discovery were equally important in improving the market's appeal.
"Good corporate governance alone does not beget investability," he said at the Institutional Investors Council Corporate Governance Conference 2026 here today.
Mohammad Faiz said raising Malaysia's investability would require coordinated action across the capital market ecosystem under the Capital Market Masterplan 2026-2030 (CMP).
Among the priorities, he said, are improving the quality of Bursa Malaysia listings to expand the investable universe, building a sustainable pipeline of initial public offering (IPO) ready companies and strengthening the corporate bond and sukuk ecosystem to provide a more seamless funding pathway for businesses.
He added that accelerating the growth of private markets, including venture capital, private equity and private credit, would further strengthen Malaysia's overall value proposition and market vibrancy.
Mohammad Faiz said boards and controlling shareholders should adopt a longer-term perspective, noting that investors are increasingly focused on sustainable value creation.
"Today, investors are asking a more fundamental question: How does governance translate into long-term value creation?" he said.
He said the SC's ongoing review of the Malaysian Code on Corporate Governance (MCCG) would place greater emphasis on long-term value creation, disciplined capital allocation, resilience and oversight of emerging risks, including technology and artificial intelligence (AI).
Mohammad Faiz also urged listed companies to strengthen investor engagement and communication.
This objective is reflected in the recently launched MY Value Up Programme, a joint initiative by the SC and Bursa Malaysia under the CMP's market vibrancy pillar.
The programme encourages companies to clearly articulate how they create value, allocate capital, measure performance and communicate their strategies to investors.
However, he noted that stronger disclosure alone would not create a more vibrant market without active participation from investors.
"Disclosure alone does not create better and more vibrant markets. Market discipline does," he said.
Mohammad Faiz called on institutional investors to play a more active stewardship role by ensuring corporate strategies are credible and management delivers on their commitments.
He noted that institutional investors accounted for about 43 per cent of equity market trading as at May 29, 2026, giving them significant influence over corporate governance through engagement, voting, escalation and capital allocation.
He said stronger stewardship would improve governance, enhance capital allocation efficiency and ultimately make Malaysia's capital market more investable.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: KJ: BN must engage Pas supporters to secure turnout at polls
Article:
BATU PAHAT: The Umno and Barisan Nasional (BN) machinery must actively engage with Pas voters, supporters and members in constituencies where the Islamist party is not contesting to ensure they turn out to vote for the coalition.
Former Umno Youth chief Khairy Jamaluddin said this was crucial amid concerns that Pas supporters might stay away from the polls despite directives from the party leadership to back BN candidates.
"That is why even though they are not contesting, I ask our candidates and machinery to approach them so they will come out to vote.
"We are worried they might not vote because there is no candidate from their own party, so it is very important for us to engage with them.
"While we welcome the statement from the Pas leadership, it is not enough without ground engagement," he said after the Lunch Hour with KJ vs NS programme, which was also attended by BN Parit Yaani candidate Datuk Najib Samuri, in Parit Besar here today.
"We have to look for them ourselves. We cannot assume that a statement from the Pas leadership means their voters will automatically support BN.
"We must put in our own effort to meet, appeal and win over their support," he said.
Khairy said he hoped the strategy would translate into stronger support for BN candidates, including from fence-sitters and Pas supporters.
At the same time, he urged BN machinery to continue engaging non-Malay voters who may be dissatisfied with the federal leadership.
"This time, we are also hoping for increased support from non-Malay voters. It is important because we do not want to be too one-sided.
"We need to ensure that, for example, in Parit Yaani, where about 45 per cent of voters are non-Malay, we reach out to as many of them as possible.
"There is disappointment among non-Malay voters, especially Chinese voters, towards PH and DAP. But disappointment with one party does not automatically mean support for another.
"Therefore, over these 10 days, we must ensure those dissatisfied with PH or DAP can be convinced to give BN a chance.
"If we execute this well in areas like Parit Yaani, God willing, we will achieve a better result than in the previous election," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Four decades on, Pahang Sultan recounts tragic Pekan school fire
Article:
KUANTAN: Some four decades after a tragic school fire in Pekan, the Sultan of Pahang today recalled an incident that has remained etched in his memory.
Speaking at the opening of the Sungai Lembing fire and rescue station, Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri'ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah said the tragic incident, which occurred in the early 1980s when he had just returned from abroad, still lingers in his mind to this day.
"Speaking about the fire and rescue department, I feel compelled to share a story involving me and them during my younger days. It occurred during the fasting month of Ramadan in Pekan.
"I was awake for sahur (pre-dawn meal) when informed that there had been a fire at a secondary school, which had just begun its new term one or two days earlier. I went to the scene and saw one block of the building burning," he said.
Al-Sultan Abdullah said firefighters had already evacuated about 16 or 17 students from the block and brought them to safety.
"My instinct told me to instruct the firemen to call out the students' names, and one name was missing. I asked the school management and was told the student had not returned from his hometown that night...but something in me told the firefighters to conduct one final sweep.
"It was a sad moment when the charred remains of the student were found curled up under a bed. It was heartbreaking...his remains was carried out on a stretcher," the Sultan said, adding that he would never forget the tragic incident.
Meanwhile, the ruler expressed his appreciation for firefighters for always going the extra mile across Pahang with their unwavering service and dedication.
"I have always felt closely connected to the fire and rescue department after going down to the ground with them during and after fire incidents.
"I have witnessed them working tirelessly, always on the front line, whether in fire incidents, search and rescue or road accidents," he said.
Al-Sultan Abdullah said the Sungai Lembing fire station is the 29th built in Pahang, and he hopes more stations will be opened in the near future.
"I was informed that new stations will be built in Gohtong Jaya (Bentong), Pulau Tioman (Rompin), and Tanjung Lumpur (Kuantan). I also hope a volunteer fire brigade will be established in Kampung Bantal in Jerantut, which is located deep in the interior.
"It will make it easier in case of any emergencies. Kampung Bantal in Ulu Tembeling is the state's first royal village, which remains close to my heart," he said.
Also present were Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail, deputy Economy Minister Datuk Mohd Shahar Abdullah, who is also Paya Besar member of parliament, deputy Housing and Local Government Minister Datuk Aiman Athirah Sabu and Fire and Rescue Department director-general Datuk Seri Nor Hisham Mohammad.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Teacher claims trial to injuring 7-year-old pupil with whiteboard eraser
Article:
BATU PAHAT: A 47-year-old female teacher pleaded not guilty at the magistrate's court here today to injuring a 7-year-old pupil by throwing a whiteboard eraser at her.
The accused, Fakhriah Bustani, claimed trial after the charge under Section 323 of the Penal Code was read out before magistrate Mustaqim Sukarno.
She was accused of intentionally causing injury to the pupil at a national school in Tongkang Pecah here, at 9am on April 1.
Section 323 provides for a jail term of up to one year, a maximum fine of RM2,000, or both, upon conviction.
Police investigations revealed that the accused allegedly threw the eraser because the victim failed to pay attention during class, striking her below the right eye and causing a bruise.
Deputy public prosecutor Firdaus Ruslan appeared for the prosecution, while the accused was represented by Normaahani Rais from the National Legal Aid Foundation.
The court allowed bail at RM1,500 and set Aug 21 for mention.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: Maszlee to campaign in all areas in Puteri Wangsa
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Pakatan Harapan candidate for Puteri Wangsa, Dr Maszlee Malik, is not intimidated by campaigning in any polling district centre in the constituency, including areas considered difficult for PH to penetrate.
Describing campaign time as the best opportunity to meet voters and present his message, he said PH has never categorised any polling district centre as either safe or difficult.
"We do not distinguish between white, black, grey, yellow and so on.
"For us, they are the people, and we are part of them. We bring their voices and listen to their grievances.
"So far, our focus is to encourage people to come out and vote, as well as to encourage those working outside the state, in Kuala Lumpur or Singapore, to return home and vote for PH," he said when met during a campaign walkabout in Taman Setia Indah here today.
Puteri Wangsa is among the key constituencies in the Johor election, featuring a five-cornered fight between Maszlee and Barisan Nasional candidate Teow Chia Ling, Nicholas Paul Vincent of Parti Bersama Malaysia, Wang Wee Siong (independent), and Muda's Rashifa Aljunied.
Maszlee said that during the one-week campaign period, he had received various complaints from residents on local issues affecting the area.
"There are different issues, so we need to address them both at the micro and holistic levels. At the holistic level, I look at Johor as a whole, while some issues can be resolved locally at the micro level.
"But many issues involve the state government and require cooperation with the federal government, such as highway works involving the Public Works Department.
"A state government that is aligned with the federal government will have a wider capacity to resolve these matters," he said.
He also said that if PH is given the mandate to govern Johor after the election, the coalition does not want people to have to queue for government aid.
"We do not want fathers or uncles to have to queue for long hours just to receive RM200, when they were promised RM500.
"For us in PH, we will improve this and not allow people to stand in the heat to receive aid.
"We will ensure payments are credited directly into bank accounts because the people have dignity.
"For PH, the people have dignity, face and self-respect. We respect the people and do not want them to queue for long hours to receive assistance," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nearly 20 million QR clearances recorded at Johor CIQs, says Saifuddin
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Nearly 20 million QR code transactions have been recorded at Johor's two main land checkpoints since the rollout of the MyNIISe system, says Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail.
He said the digital initiative had helped ease congestion at the Malaysia-Singapore border.
"As of Sunday (June 28), the MyNIISe application had recorded 2.4 million downloads and 1.27 million users, while 19.48 million QR code transactions were recorded at both the Customs, Immigration and Quarantine (CIQ) complexes at Bangunan Sultan Iskandar (BSI) and Kompleks Sultan Abu Bakar (KSAB).
"People used to talk about congestion at the Johor Causeway. Today, I want to share the solution.
"The MyNIISe application is now more stable, and more people are using the QR facility to enter and leave the country through BSI and KSAB.
"This shows more travellers are choosing faster, more convenient digital lanes, helping to improve traffic flow at the country's two main entry points," he said in a Facebook post today.
Saifuddin said the digital transformation had also been expanded to five major airports nationwide, where more than 5.59 million QR transactions had been recorded to date.
He said the initiative reflected the government's commitment to delivering tangible improvements in public services through digital transformation.
"Reforms should not merely be announced but translated into services that are more efficient, faster and directly benefit the people.
"Digital initiatives such as MyNIISe will continue to be strengthened to improve service delivery, simplify daily travel and enhance the efficiency of the country's immigration system," he said.
The Johor-Singapore land crossings via the Johor Causeway (BSI) and the Second Link (KSAB) are among the busiest international checkpoints globally, driven largely by daily commuter movement between Johor and Singapore.
Combined, both crossings typically handle around half a million travellers daily, with numbers surging past 500,000 during peak holidays and long weekends.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: Muar district office denies barring Anwar from using govt complex
Article:
MUAR: The Muar district office today denied claims that Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim was barred from using the Penghulu Mukim Sungai Balang Complex for a community engagement programme last month.
Muar district officer Zainal Eran said the event was relocated following administrative concerns over an incomplete application and the risk of creating a political perception.
"The district administration never intended to prevent the prime minister from meeting fishermen, farmers, entrepreneurs and educators during his visit on June 15."
Instead, he said, the programme proceeded as scheduled after all parties agreed to relocate it to Pantai Leka Food Court Square, a venue deemed more suitable.
"The issue was never about preventing the prime minister from holding the programme.
"It was an administrative decision based on incomplete information received by the district office, the need for proper coordination, and the responsibility to avoid any perception that a state government facility was being used for political activities," Zainal said in a statement today.
He reiterated that Anwar was never barred from using the government facility, but the district office did not receive any formal application for the use of the Sungai Balang Complex.
Instead, the office received only a telephone call from the Johor Development Office under the Prime Minister's Department on the morning of June 14 informing it of plans to use the complex the following day.
However, Zainal said no detailed information on the programme's purpose, itinerary or invitation arrangements was provided, making it difficult for the district administration to coordinate preparations.
He said the district office also had legitimate concerns that the event could be perceived as having political elements, prompting a precautionary decision not to approve the use of the state-owned premises.
"The programme itself was welcomed and was never cancelled. An alternative venue was proposed and agreed upon by both the district administration and the Johor Development Office," he said.
Zainal said the event was successfully held at Pantai Leka at 5.30pm on June 15 and was attended by federal government leaders, party leaders, officers from relevant agencies and members of the local community.
He added that developments on the day of the programme reinforced the district office's earlier concerns.
Zainal said political party flags were erected along roads near Pantai Leka on the morning of the event, prompting the local council to remove them. The flags were re-erected after the event concluded.
He also said that a political gathering and 'ceramah' were held in Muar later that night, although the official election campaign period had yet to begin.
"These developments strengthened the district office's initial assessment that allowing the use of a state government building could have created the perception of political activity," he said.
Zainal urged all parties not to politicise the issue, saying the decision fell within the district administration's discretionary powers and did not affect the smooth running of the prime minister's programme or public harmony.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Couple arrested over viral Federal Highway hit-and-run crash
Article:
PETALING JAYA: Police arrested a married couple in connection with a three-vehicle crash on the Federal Highway here that went viral after the driver allegedly fled⁠ the scene.
Petaling Jaya police chief Assistant Commissioner Shamsudin Mamat said the Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Division detained the couple, both in their 50s, about 9pm yesterday over the June 20 incident involving a Perodua Kembara.
The case stems from a viral dashcam video showing a reckless
"brake-check"
incident on the Federal Highway towards Kuala Lumpur from Shah Alam about 6pm on June 20.
In the footage, the Kembara driver is seen repeatedly braking suddenly in front of another motorist before a collision occurs. The driver then allegedly fled the scene, leaving the vehicle behind.
The video, circulated widely on social media, triggered public outrage and calls for police action.
"The magistrate granted a four-day remand on the first suspect from July 2 until July 5, while the second suspect was released on police bail after her statement was recorded.
"The investigation paper will be referred to the Deputy Public Prosecutor's Office for further instructions," he said in a statement today.
The injured motorcyclist involved in the incident was taken to Sunway Hospital for treatment.
The case is being investigated under Section 42(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 for reckless and dangerous driving.
Shamsudin reminded road users to obey traffic laws, warning that police would continue taking firm action against offenders.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Navy chief: More than 60pct of fleet now over 40 years old
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN) is operating with an increasingly ageing fleet, with more than 60 per cent of its vessels now over 40 years old.
Its chief Admiral Tan Sri Zulhelmy Ithnain said the situation had become one of the navy's most pressing operational challenges, driven by rising maintenance costs, difficulty sourcing spare parts and concerns over long-term seaworthiness.
"More than 60 per cent of our assets are over 40 years old. They are obsolete. This is one of the navy's biggest challenges today.
"We have six operations that require ships to be deployed 24 hours a day throughout the year.
"These ageing ships still have to go to sea, and as the leadership, we are concerned because their condition is no longer the same as when they first entered service," he said after the 53rd anniversary gathering of the RMN's First Fast Strike Craft Squadron.
Zulhelmy said the navy continuously assessed the seaworthiness of its ships but acknowledged that many vessels should already have been replaced.
He said older ships increasingly required corrective maintenance rather than preventive maintenance, resulting in higher costs and more frequent breakdowns.
"Once ships become obsolete, corrective maintenance overtakes preventive maintenance. They no longer fail according to routine schedules; they simply break down due to age.
"Spare parts are also becoming increasingly difficult to obtain," he said.
He added that many vessels were already beyond economical repair, but the navy had no choice but to keep them in service until replacements arrived.
Referring to the Fast Strike Craft, which has been in service since 1973, Zulhelmy said many countries operating similar vessels had already retired them, leaving Malaysia among the few still maintaining such ageing platforms.
"They entered service 53 years ago and have since been downgraded to patrol vessels after their missile systems became obsolete.
"Two vessels, KD Perdana and KD Ganas, are currently undergoing an obsolescence programme to strengthen their hulls and extend their service lives by another 10 to 15 years.
"This is only an interim measure. We cannot continue indefinitely as maintenance costs will keep rising," he said.
Zulhelmy said the RMN remained committed to its 15-to-5 Transformation Programme, which aims to replace older vessels with fewer but more modern ship classes in phases.
He said procurement would proceed according to government financial capacity, under a phased replacement plan.
He added that five ships had already been decommissioned as part of the transition, while awaiting delivery of new assets, including the Littoral Combat Ships (LCS) and Littoral Mission Ship (LMS) Batch 2.
"As a maritime nation with sea boundaries more than twice our land area, we need the right assets to carry out our responsibilities effectively," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid backs proposal for Pengerang to become new district
Article:
KOTA TINGGI: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has voiced his support for a proposal to designate Pengerang as a new administrative district alongside Kota Tinggi.
He said Pengerang's economic growth and population increase also required more comprehensive basic facilities, including the construction of a hospital to meet local needs.
He said both proposals put forward by Pengerang member of parliament Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said should be given serious consideration as they would directly benefit the people.
"One, a district. Pengerang district. Of course I would not disagree. Another request is for a hospital, and I am even more in agreement.
"But if both receive stronger support, they will be implemented sooner," he said when speaking at the 2025 Excellent Service Award Ceremony and Santuni Kasih programme by the South Johor Development Authority (Kejora) here today.
At the same time, Zahid said Kejora plans to expand its development jurisdiction to more than 300,000 hectares in an effort to strengthen development in the Johor Selatan region.
Zahid, who is also rural and regional development minister, said the proposal had received positive feedback based on a survey, with about 96 per cent of respondents agreeing to the expansion.
"A study was conducted to obtain a baseline of agreement, and about 96 per cent of respondents agreed for Kejora to expand its development area, subject to the consent of the Sultan of Johor and the Regent of Johor," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Musk to remain world's only dollar trillionaire for long time, says US expert
Article:
WASHINGTON: The world is unlikely to see new dollar trillionaires any time soon after SpaceX chief executive officer Elon Musk became the first to cross the US$1 trillion (RM4 trillion) personal net worth threshold, Jay Ritter, professor of finance at the University of Florida's Warrington College of Business, told RIA Novosti.
"We may have to wait for a very long time before anyone else becomes a trillionaire," Ritter said.
He also noted that there is no data yet suggesting that anyone else in the world has a fortune significantly exceeding US$300 billion, with the top seven richest people in the media rankings being founders of US technology companies.
"Many of these people are planning to donate part or most of their wealth to charities. Bill Gates, the co-founder of Microsoft, has already given away most of his wealth, which is why he is not among the top seven," the expert said.
When such rankings are developed, only the market value of shares owned by billionaires is taken into account, and not tax obligations. If Amazon founder Jeff Bezos sold all his shares, he would have to pay taxes, which would affect his fortune, Ritter noted.
Musk became the world's first trillionaire in history after his SpaceX rocket and AI company went public on June 12.
Based on Wednesday's Bloomberg Billionaires Index data, Musk remains the world's richest person, with his wealth growing by US$391 billion this year to US$1.01 trillion. Google co-founders Larry Page and Sergey Brin rank second and third, with their wealth rising by US$32.2 billion to US$301 billion and by US$29.9 billion to US$280 billion, respectively.
Amazon founder Jeff Bezos is in fourth place with US$262 billion, up US$8.42 billion. Dell Technologies founder Michael Dell rounds out the top five with US$217 billion, up US$77.1 billion. Oracle co-founder Larry Ellison, who held fifth place last month, has dropped to sixth after his wealth fell by US$44.9 billion to US$202 billion. - BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: Pas move not automatic BN advantage in Johor polls, says Zahid
Article:
KOTA TINGGI: Pas' move urging its supporters not to vote for Pakatan Harapan in seats contested by the opposition coalition should not be seen as an automatic advantage for Barisan Nasional in the Johor election.
BN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said BN would focus entirely on the strength and credibility of its candidates in the July 11 polls.
"That is their (Pas) approach. We move professionally by focusing on our candidates, though any additional support is certainly welcome.
"We want to see the results on July 11 when the ballot boxes are opened.
"That will be the concrete proof of whether what they say holds true or otherwise," he said at a press conference after the 2025 Excellent Service Award and Santunan Kasih Kejora ceremony at Lotus Desaru here today.
Also present was Pengerang member of parliament Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said, who is also Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform).
It was previously reported that Pas had urged voters not to cast their ballots for PH in the Johor polls, including in constituencies involving straight fights between BN and PH.
Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang had said that the directive was among five resolutions decided during a special central Pas working committee meeting last Monday.
On claims by certain quarters that the Johor election campaign has been dull, Zahid dismissed the notion.
"A dull atmosphere might be the interpretation of other parties, but BN feels the campaign is currently in full swing.
"Whatever stance other coalitions take towards BN is their right, whether to support us or otherwise.
"Naturally, we would be grateful if that support translates into votes, helping secure victory for BN candidates in this Johor election," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: More than 2 million military casualties in Ukraine war: Study
Article:
WASHINGTON: Russia's invasion of Ukraine has caused more than two million military casualties, with Moscow's forces bearing the brunt of the losses, according to a study published Wednesday by a US think tank.
"Combined Russian and Ukrainian casualties have exceeded 2 million," the Center for Strategic and International Studies (CSIS) said.
It estimated that between 400,000 and 450,000 Russians have been killed since Moscow invaded Ukraine in February 2022, out of a total of 1.4 million casualties – killed, wounded and missing – among Russian troops.
Ukrainian forces have meanwhile suffered between 525,000 and 625,000 casualties and between 125,000 and 150,000 fatalities during the same period, CSIS said.
"Russian fatalities in Ukraine are more than four times greater than all US fatalities in all wars combined since World War II," while the ratio of Russian to Ukrainian casualties has likely risen to around 8 to 1 in the first half of this year, it added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man rescued unharmed as fire guts 2 shophouses in Penang
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: A man was rescued unharmed after a fire gutted two three-storey shophouses housing a restaurant in Jalan Perak here today.
The fire, reported at 12.32pm, involved two permanent three-storey shophouses, with the first unit sustaining about 90 per cent damage and the second about 80 per cent.
A team from the Jalan Perak Fire and Rescue Station, assisted by personnel from the Lebuh Pantai station, arrived at the scene a minute after receiving the emergency call.
The blaze was brought under control at 12.44pm, though firefighting operations were still ongoing at the time of reporting.
The victim, a man believed to be in his 30s or 40s, was rescued by firefighters from the building and was unhurt.
Penang Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations John Sagun Francis said 11 personnel, including supervisors, were deployed for the operation, supported by a Fire Rescue Tender (FRT), Emergency Medical Rescue Services (EMRS) unit, Light Fire Rescue Tender (LFRT) and an Aerial Ladder Platform (ALP).
The cause of the fire and losses are being investigated.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Saifuddin wins bid to uphold default judgment in defamation suit
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail has succeeded in upholding a default judgment in his defamation suit against a social media user over posts linked to Kedah politics and Kedah Darul Aman FC.
The Alor Star High Court dismissed Muhammad Zubair Ismail's application to set aside the default judgment after finding that he had failed to establish a defence warranting a trial.
High Court Judge Dr Mohammad Johan Lee, in grounds of judgment dated June 30, ruled that the defendant had failed to file a statement of defence despite being served via substituted service.
"The defendant failed to raise a credible defence for trial after disregarding the substituted service mechanism. Accordingly, the application is dismissed with costs of RM8,000 to the plaintiff," he said.
The court also noted that Zubair had not attached a draft statement of defence to his application, preventing the court from assessing the merits of his proposed case.
The judge further found the defendant's affidavit to be contradictory.
While Zubair claimed the impugned posts were true, he also said he could not recall or verify their contents as his devices had been seized by the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission and his social media accounts blocked.
"The court is of the view that a person cannot assert that a statement is true while simultaneously claiming not to know its contents," he said.
The judge also rejected the argument that Saifuddin was not explicitly named, ruling that the minister was identifiable through posters bearing his image and references to the nicknames "Pudin" and "Pudin Pokka" in videos related to Kedah politics.
Saifuddin's lawyer, Datuk S.N. Nair, said the ruling meant the default judgment remained valid and binding.
He said the case would now proceed to the assessment of damages stage.
The suit arose from Facebook and TikTok posts and videos published in March 2025 alleging misconduct involving Saifuddin in relation to Kedah politics and KDA FC.
Court documents were served via substituted service after two failed attempts at personal service. The court entered default judgment on Dec 8, 2025 after the defendant failed to enter appearance or file a defence.
Zubair later applied to set aside the judgment, claiming he only became aware of it through news reports and insisting he had not received the court documents, while maintaining the posts were true or constituted fair comment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: SoftBank's LY, Bain raise Kakaku bid again with US$4.1bil valuation
Article:
TOKYO: SoftBank's LY Corp and Bain Capital have again raised their bid for Kakaku.com , valuing the Japanese price-comparison website operator at 670 billion yen (US$4.12 billion) and widening the gap over a rival offer from Sweden's EQT.
In the legally binding offer unveiled late on Wednesday, LY and Bain said they would raise their bid for all outstanding Kakaku.com shares to 3,384 yen apiece from 3,232 yen each proposed in May.
Following the new proposal, Kakaku.com said in a statement on Thursday that it would pursue discussions with EQT over its offer price, currently at 3,000 yen a share, while maintaining its support for the Swedish investment firm's bid.
It withdrew its shareholder recommendation to support the EQT bid, changing its stance to "neutral," adding that it would engage in talks with both bidders.
M&A activity has surged in Japan in response to changes in governance that have encouraged more firms to go private. Private equity firms have spearheaded a number of deals, and last year KKR and Bain battled to take private Fuji Soft, a software developer. KKR ultimately prevailed in that faceoff.
LY and Bain said their offer would increase to 3,500 yen a share if KDDI Corp, one of Kakaku.com's largest shareholders, agreed to support the bid.
EQT said in May, following the initial announcement of the rival bid, that it was confident in the attractiveness of its offer. EQT did not respond to a request for comment on Thursday.
Kakaku operates a range of online businesses including price-comparison website Kakaku.com, restaurant review and reservation platform Tabelog and job-search service Kyujin Box.
Bain and LY said they could improve the profitability of Kakaku's businesses by injecting capital, providing management support and aligning with LY's existing businesses, which include the Line messaging app and Yahoo Japan.
LY and Bain said that they would not launch their offer unless they received an opinion in support from Kakaku.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Liew: Malaysia must boost women's workforce participation to achieve high-income status
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia must increase women's participation in the workforce if it hopes to accelerate income growth and achieve high-income nation status, Deputy Finance Minister Datuk Seri Liew Chin Tong said today.
Citing the latest figures released by Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar, Liew said women now make up almost 60 per cent of Malaysia's civil service, with about 43 per cent holding leadership positions — among the highest rates globally for a Muslim-majority country.
However, he said female labour force participation in the broader economy remained comparatively low, representing one of Malaysia's biggest untapped opportunities for economic growth.
"For a Muslim-majority nation, these are very high numbers. But when we compare them with female labour force participation, especially with our regional neighbours, there is still a considerable gap that we need to address," he said.
Liew called on the private sector, particularly business chambers, to take the lead in creating workplaces that encourage more women to enter and remain in formal employment.
"The quickest way to raise household income is to have two income earners instead of one. We need to find ways to ensure more Malaysian women participate in the workforce and remain in the formal economy longer."
He cited Japan as an example, saying its higher female labour force participation over the past 15 years had contributed significantly to the country's economic expansion.
Liew delivered the opening keynote address at the sixth National Economic Forum (NEF 2026), organised by the National Chamber of Commerce and Industry of Malaysia (NCCIM) at the Kuala Lumpur Golf and Country Club Convention Centre.
Held under the theme, A World in Transition: Securing Malaysia's Economic Future in an Era of Disruption, the forum attracted nearly 600 participants, including business leaders, policymakers, government officials, diplomats, economists, academics, investors and industry representatives from across the country.
His keynote address, titled Securing Malaysia's Future in a World in Transition: Strengthening Resilience, Confidence and Competitiveness, set the tone for discussions on Malaysia's long-term economic priorities amid an increasingly uncertain global landscape.
The forum also featured a keynote presentation by Malaysian Inclusive Development and Advancement Institute (Minda) director Professor Tan Sri Datuk Seri Dr Noor Azlan Ghazali, alongside three plenary sessions covering institutional resilience, workforce readiness, geopolitical developments, trade realignment, investment competitiveness and innovation.
Responding to questions from participants after his speech, Liew said Malaysia must place greater emphasis on ensuring foreign direct investment (FDI) generates tangible benefits for local businesses and workers instead of focusing solely on attracting investment commitments.
He said the government's new investment incentive framework, introduced on March 1, aims to evaluate investments based on measurable outcomes, including technology transfer, quality employment, local supply chain participation and the development of Malaysian companies.
"For many years, we focused on attracting investment. Going forward, we must also nurture these investments and monitor whether they create value for Malaysian companies, Malaysian workers and the broader economy," he said.
Liew said foreign investment should not be viewed as an end in itself but as a catalyst for developing stronger Malaysian enterprises, including small and medium enterprises (SMEs) and future multinational corporations.
Now in its sixth edition, the National Economic Forum has become NCCIM's flagship annual platform for dialogue between the government and the private sector, bringing together policymakers and industry leaders to deliberate on Malaysia's economic challenges and opportunities, as well as contribute recommendations to national policymaking.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia must grow economy to tackle household debt, says Liew
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia must boost economic growth and incomes, alongside responsible lending, to tackle household debt, which stood at 84.4 per cent of gross domestic product (GDP) in March.
Deputy Finance Minister Datuk Seri Liew Chin Tong said household debt stood at RM1.73 trillion and had remained above 80 per cent for almost 15 years following the global financial crisis.
"It has been at this level since the period after the 2008 financial crisis, when interest rates around the world were exceptionally low."
He said the issue should be viewed from two perspectives.
"One is ensuring that borrowing and lending remain responsible. The other is growing the economy.
"If GDP expands significantly, then the existing level of debt becomes smaller relative to the size of the economy," he said in response to a question on the government's plans to address high household debt.
Liew said there was no single approach to tackling the issue.
"Malaysia needed to accelerate economic growth while ensuring wages increased alongside productivity.
"We need Malaysians to be paid better and Malaysian companies to become more competitive. When incomes rise and the economy grows faster, household debt will become more manageable," he said after delivering a keynote address at the National Economic Forum 2026 organised by the National Chamber of Commerce and Industry of Malaysia today.
He said the government had established the Consumer Credit Commission to strengthen oversight of consumer financing, including the rapidly expanding buy now, pay later sector.
"The commission will help ensure consumer lending remains responsible."
However, he stressed that long-term debt sustainability ultimately depended on Malaysia achieving faster and more equitable economic growth.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Super Micro says two Taiwan staff detained in probe involving its AI servers
Article:
TAIPEI: Super Micro said on Wednesday that two employees at its Taiwan unit were detained pending a court hearing, while two others were released on bail in a probe into alleged illegal exports of AI servers containing Nvidia chips.
The servers are made by Super Micro and contain Nvidia chips, which are subject to US export controls prohibiting export to China.
The four workers were among six people questioned earlier this week when Taiwan's Keelung District Prosecutors' Office said it had launched a second round of searches in the probe.
The six were questioned over alleged document forgery and breach of trust, it said, adding searches were conducted at 12 locations, including the homes of six suspects and the offices of three companies.
The companies searched were Super Micro Taiwan, Albatron Technology, Super Micro's distributor in Taiwan, and Chief Telecom, a data centre operator.
In a letter to customers issued in the United States on Wednesday, Super Micro chief revenue officer Matthew Thauberger said the four employees had been questioned on June 29 in connection with what he described as a Taiwanese investigation regarding the company's sale of products to a technology company in Taiwan.
"Two of the four employees have been detained pending a hearing, and the other two have been released on bail," Thauberger wrote in the letter.
"Super Micro is not a target of this investigation," he said, adding that the company had been working with Taiwanese authorities for several months.
Thauberger said the company had provided Taiwan authorities access to the employees' desks and electronic devices and had immediately placed all four employees on administrative leave, pending the outcome of the investigation.
In May, Taiwanese prosecutors launched the first round of the investigation, detaining three people suspected of illegally exporting Super Micro's high-end AI servers, equipped with the Nvidia chips. Those three remain in detention.
In a statement issued in May, Super Micro said it had been cooperating with Taiwan authorities in an investigation into the alleged diversion of its AI servers to the restricted Chinese market.
The cooperation had led to the seizure of 50 servers, the company said, adding that they had been deceptively acquired after being sold to an authorised reseller.
In March, the US Justice Department charged three people associated with Super Micro, including one of its co-founders, with helping smuggle at least US$2.5 billion worth of US AI technology to China in violation of US export laws.
Semiconductor powerhouse Taiwan is the world's largest producer of advanced chips used in AI applications.
Taiwan has tightened export controls in recent years to prevent advanced technology and know-how from reaching China, which claims the democratically governed island as its own territory despite Taiwan's strong objections.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Domestic reform key to global contributions, says Anwar
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said Malaysia must first put its own house in order by strengthening governance, stressing that the country's ability to make meaningful contributions on the global stage depends on successful domestic reform.
Speaking during a question-and-answer session at the 39th Asia-Pacific Roundtable today, Anwar said his government's priority is to improve governance, eradicate corruption, reduce inequality, and ensure every citizen has access to quality education and healthcare.
He said while Malaysia will continue taking principled positions on international issues, its immediate focus remains on building a better nation and demonstrating that a Muslim-majority country can uphold democratic values, moderation, and inclusivity.
"I would like to make sure that I can effect change and reform in my country. Let us use our power to champion the cause of all common men and women and ensure every citizen has the right to basic education and health," he said.
Anwar also cautioned against viewing global divisions as a 'clash of civilisations', arguing instead that many conflicts stem from misunderstanding and prejudice.
Quoting the late Palestinian-American scholar Edward Said, the prime minister described many international tensions as a "clash of ignorance", saying people often formed judgments about others without genuine understanding.
He said Malaysia's Madani framework places equal emphasis on technological advancement and human values, encouraging greater appreciation among people of different races, religions, and cultures.
Referring to the Quranic concept of 'li ta'arafu', Anwar said societies should strive not merely to tolerate one another but to understand and appreciate their differences, adding that such an approach is especially important for a multiracial and multireligious country like Malaysia.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pahang Sultan calls for river dredging to strengthen flood mitigation
Article:
KUANTAN: Building embankment walls without deepening riverbeds will be ineffective in preventing floods, the Sultan of Pahang, Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri'ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah, said.
The ruler reiterated his call for shallow rivers to be deepened, noting that embankments would be of little use if rivers continued to silt up.
"I have always emphasised that any village or town located along a river needs to have the river flowing through it deepened. No matter how high the embankments are, there is no use if the river is laden with silt and continues to become shallow.
"Our rivers are becoming shallower, and the water will eventually overflow into the surrounding areas. It is a very simple formula… no rocket science is needed," he said in his speech when opening the RM6 million Sungai Lembing Fire and Rescue Station here today.
Also present were Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail, Deputy Economy Minister Datuk Mohd Shahar Abdullah, who is also the Paya Besar member of parliament and Deputy Housing and Local Government Minister Datuk Aiman Athirah Sabu and Fire and Rescue Department director-general Datuk Seri Nor Hisham Mohammad.
Al-Sultan Abdullah expressed gratitude to the state and federal governments for allocating substantial funds for infrastructure in Pahang, especially the RM100 million Sungai Lembing flood mitigation project.
The project, which began in 2024, is scheduled for completion by early next year.
"These projects are necessary because Sungai Lembing is among the areas that experience flooding almost every year.
"If you want to know whether Pahang is going to flood, look at Sungai Lembing first. If Sungai Lembing floods, then Kuantan will likely flood as well....that has long been a benchmark for us in Pahang," he said.
Early last year, Al-Sultan Abdullah called for the deepening and widening of rivers across the state, saying sedimentation had caused rivers in several districts to become shallower, resulting in frequent overflows and flooding.
On Sungai Lembing, a former tin-mining hub, Al-Sultan Abdullah noted that the town has a rich history that thrived during the British colonial era before economic shifts led to its decline.
"It was once a thriving mining town during the British colonial era. However, mining activities eventually declined due to economic and market conditions at the time," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hardware store worker charged with raping, sexually assaulting teenage sister
Article:
KUALA TERENGGANU: A 25-year-old hardware store worker pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court here to charges of raping and sexually assaulting his 16-year-old biological sister.
The accused claimed trial after both charges were read out before judge Mohd Zul Zakiqudin Zulkifli.
The court denied bail following an application by the prosecution, citing the sibling relationship and the need to protect the victim from potential interference.
For the first charge, the man is accused of raping his underage sister in January around 11pm at a house in Kampung Titian Baru.
The charge was framed under Section 376(3) of the Penal Code for incestuous rape, which carries a prison sentence of between eight and 30 years and at least 10 strokes of the rotan upon conviction.
For the second charge, the accused allegedly committed physical sexual assault against the victim at the same location around 9pm on June 21.
As the victim's biological brother, he was charged under Section 14(d) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017, read together with Section 16(1) of the same act for breaching a position of trust.
Section 14(d) carries a maximum prison sentence of 20 years and whipping, while Section 16(1) provides for an additional prison term of up to five years and a minimum of two strokes of the rotan.
The court fixed Aug 2 for mention.
Deputy public prosecutor Muhammad Asymawi Ghazali prosecuted, while the accused was represented by a lawyer from the National Legal Aid Foundation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mat Sabu to represent Malaysia at Ali Khamenei's funeral in Teheran
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu will represent Malaysia at the funeral of Iran's former Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei in Teheran tomorrow.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said he had been invited to attend the ceremony but was unable to do so due to prior commitments.
"For the funeral of Khamenei tomorrow in Teheran, we are sending our agriculture minister to represent the country," he said during a fireside chat at the 37th Asia-Pacific Roundtable today.
Khamenei, a spiritual figure for many Shia Muslims, was
killed
at the age of 86 at his compound in central Teheran on Feb 28.
His son, Mojtaba Khamenei, succeeded him as Supreme Leader in early March, the third since the establishment of the Islamic Republic in 1979.
The funeral, initially scheduled for March but postponed due to the war, will now take place across several cities, beginning with three days of ceremonies in Teheran from July 4, followed by a service in Qom on July 7, and burial in his hometown of Mashhad.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sultan of Pahang recalls tragic school fire that still haunts him
Article:
KUANTAN: The Sultan of Pahang today shared a deeply personal recollection of a school fire incident that still lingers in his memory, when he was present at the scene alongside firefighters who later discovered the charred remains of a student under a bed.
Speaking at the opening of the Sungai Lembing Fire and Rescue Station, Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri'ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah said the tragedy in the early 1980s, shortly after his return from abroad, remained vivid to this day.
"Speaking about the Fire and Rescue Department, I feel compelled to share a story from my younger days. It happened during Ramadan in Pekan.
"I was awake for sahur when I was informed of a fire at a secondary school that had just begun its term one or two days earlier. I went to the scene and saw one block of the building on fire," he said.
Al-Sultan Abdullah said firefighters had already rescued about 16 to 17 students from the building.
"My instinct told me to ask the firefighters to call out the students' names, and one name was missing. I checked with the school management and was told the student had not returned from his hometown that night. But something told me the firefighters should conduct one final sweep.
"It was a sad moment when the charred remains of the student were found curled up under a bed. It was heartbreaking. His remains were carried out on a stretcher," he said, adding that he would never forget the incident.
The Ruler also expressed his appreciation to firefighters for their dedication and willingness to go the extra mile across Pahang.
"I have always felt closely connected to the Fire and Rescue Department after going down to the ground with them during fire incidents.
"I have witnessed them working tirelessly on the front line, whether during fires, search and rescue operations or road accidents," he said.
Al-Sultan Abdullah said the Sungai Lembing Fire and Rescue Station was the 29th in Pahang and expressed hope that more stations would be built in future.
"I was informed that new stations will be built in Gohtong Jaya, Pulau Tioman and Tanjung Lumpur. I also hope a volunteer fire brigade will be set up in Kampung Bantal, Jerantut, deep in the interior.
"It will make it easier in case of emergencies. Kampung Bantal in Ulu Tembeling is the state's first royal village, which remains close to my heart," he said.
Also present were Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail, Deputy Economy Minister Datuk Mohd Shahar Abdullah, Deputy Housing and Local Government Minister Datuk Aiman Athirah Sabu, and Fire and Rescue Department director-general Datuk Seri Nor Hisham Mohammad.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nancy: Ministry launches 18-month National Gentlemen Study to address mental health, social issues
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Women, Family and Community Development Ministry will launch the National Gentlemen Study (Kajian Lelaki Budiman Negara) this month to develop policies and initiatives aimed at empowering men.
Its minister, Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri, said the study under the 13th Malaysia Plan (13MP) will span 18 months to help nurture resilient, responsible men who contribute significantly to national and family well-being.
"The empowerment of men must be viewed from a comprehensive framework, not just from the leadership or economic aspects, but encompassing spiritual strength, emotional stability, mature thinking, and the ability to bear amanah responsibly," she said.
Nancy said this at the opening ceremony of the Men's Empowerment Consultative Forum at the Putrajaya International Convention Centre today.
She noted that modern men, particularly young fathers, face complex challenges ranging from high living costs to balancing career demands with family responsibilities.
"Many struggle to adapt as heads of households without sufficient support. These challenges require men to be not only physically resilient, but also emotionally stable, well-versed in parenting skills, and mentally fit," she said.
Nancy cited Health Ministry statistics showing that the suicide rate among men is nearly three times higher than that of women.
Furthermore, the National Health and Morbidity Survey 2023 reported that 4.6 per cent of the population aged 16 and above suffer from depression, including men.
"At the same time, economic pressures continue to impact family well-being. Bank Negara Malaysia reports show household debt stands at 84.3 per cent of GDP, while divorce cases increased by 4.1 per cent to 60,457 cases in 2024," she said.
She added that the police statistics on domestic violence from January to December last year revealed that 95 per cent of the perpetrators were men.
Consequently, she said men's empowerment must expand beyond financial provision to include character development, mental health, financial literacy, parenting skills, and strong social support networks.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: Bersatu members backing PN rivals committing sabotage, says Tun Faisal
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Bersatu has slammed party members who threw their support behind political parties contesting against Perikatan Nasional in the Johor election.
Its information chief, Datuk Tun Faisal Ismail Aziz, said any action that could affect the chances of victory of any PN candidate may be regarded as a form of sabotage.
He said Bersatu does not support PH or BN in the July 11 polls.
"There is no decision by the PN Supreme Council, nor any official PN policy, instructing or allowing support for PH or BN.
"As such, Bersatu adheres to this policy decision," said Tun Faisal in a statement on Facebook.
Although he did not single out anyone, it is believed that Tun Faisal was referring to reports of Sri Medan Bersatu members who had announced their support for the PH candidate contesting the seat.
PH is fielding Hishammuddin @ Misrin Ishak in a three-cornered fight for the seat. BN is fielding Datuk Zulkurnain Kamisen, while PN is fielding Ahmad Rosdi Bahari of Pas.
"All members and supporters must give full support to every candidate contesting under the PN logo.
"This policy decision serves as an official guideline and is binding on all Bersatu members and PN component parties.
"Any action that could affect the chances of victory of any PN candidate may be regarded as a form of sabotage against the joint efforts of PN component parties in facing the Johor election," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Weak referral system leads to rising demand, cost pressures: Private hospitals body
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's healthcare system is facing a structural gap in primary care integration with private hospital stakeholders warning that general practitioners (GPs) are not being effectively used as gatekeepers, leading to unnecessary hospital utilisation.
Association of Private Hospitals Malaysia (APHM) honorary secretary Anwar Anis said the weak primary care referral system is contributing to rising demand and cost pressures across the healthcare sector.
He said many patients bypass GP clinics and head straight to hospitals for conditions that could be treated at primary care level, resulting in avoidable congestion.
"This contributes to overutilisation in the system because many cases that reach hospitals could actually be managed by GPs," he said.
Anwar was speaking on the opportunities and challenges faced by private hospitals at the Bursa-HLIB 21st Stratum Focus Series themed "Malaysia's Healthcare Industry Moving Forward."
Anwar said the World Bank findings support the view that rising healthcare costs are driven mainly by higher utilisation rather than treatment price increases.
He said the data shows utilisation growth is higher than earlier estimates and makes up most of medical inflation.
He also cited ageing populations and rising non-communicable diseases (NCDs) as key drivers of increased hospital visits.
"When we talk about a 15 per cent increase, about 60-70 per cent of that is actually driven by utilisation. This means medical inflation is often misunderstood, as it is not mainly caused by higher procedure costs.
"It is not that the price of treatment has increased by 15 per cent. The main factor is more patients coming in due to ageing and NCDs," he added.
Anwar said this trend is adding pressure to healthcare reforms, including the Medical and Health Insurance Takaful (MHIT) framework and Diagnosis-Related Group (DRG) payment system.
He said these reforms aim to improve transparency and cost control but do not fully address how patients enter the healthcare system.
He added that strengthening primary care is necessary to ensure the system works more efficiently.
"Even with MHIT and DRG, if patients continue to bypass primary care, we are only managing costs at hospital level. If we want to manage healthcare inflation sustainably, we must address utilisation," said Anwar.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Three foreigners arrested, RM769,480 illicit cigarettes seized in Johor Baru raid
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Police seized illicit cigarettes worth RM769,480 and arrested three foreigners in a raid on a house in Taman Daya under Op Taring Alpha 1 on Wednesday night.
The 10pm operation was carried out by the Wildlife Crime Bureau/Perisikan Siasatan Khas (WCB/PSK) under Bukit Aman's Internal Security and Public Order Department, led by Deputy Superintendent Khairul Fairoz Rodzuan, with assistance from Johor police's D7 and D9 units.
WCB/PSK commander Datuk Mohd Zaki Ashar said two Bangladeshi men, aged 26 and 49, and a 51-year-old Indonesian woman were detained.
He said police also seized 292,000 sticks of suspected duty-unpaid cigarettes of various brands from the house and a Proton Exora parked at the premises.
"The total seizure is valued at RM769,480, with the actual value of the confiscated items at RM744,480.
"The operation disrupted a storage and distribution network for duty-unpaid cigarettes operating in the Johor Baru area," he said in a statement.
Zaki said investigations were being carried out under Section 135(1)(d) of the Customs Act 1967 and Section 6(1)(c) of the Immigration Act 1959/63.
He added that efforts were ongoing to track down the mastermind believed to be behind the smuggling syndicate, who remains at large.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: India tells WhatsApp to halt usernames rollout, justify feature or face action
Article:
India has asked WhatsApp to justify the implementation of a planned feature covering usernames and to freeze the rollout in its biggest market, escalating a crackdown on messaging anonymity that began with Telegram, according to a government letter reviewed by Reuters.
Earlier this week, Meta's WhatsApp said it had begun a phased global rollout, including in India, of the feature, which lets users reserve a unique username and eventually message others without sharing their phone numbers.
The July 1 letter gave WhatsApp three days to respond and barred the rollout until its consultations with the government concluded.
Anonymity was among the concerns India cited when it temporarily blocked the messaging app Telegram last month.
The intervention is an escalation of India's policing of online platforms, coming weeks after it blocked Telegram and following years of run-ins with Elon Musk's X over content-takedown orders.
India is WhatsApp's biggest market with more than 500 million users, and the standoff forces the platform to weigh compliance against mounting concerns about expanding government control of social media.
India's IT ministry said the feature could materially increase online fraud, phishing, digital arrest scams and impersonation attacks by allowing bad actors to contact victims without disclosing their phone numbers.
"We've announced the option for people to reserve their preferred username on WhatsApp," a Meta spokesperson said, adding that the feature was not yet live and the company had reserved usernames for public figures, government entities and verified Meta accounts to help prevent impersonation.
The notice comes weeks after India scrutinised Telegram over features that let users interact without disclosing their numbers. It also comes after a June home ministry report, reviewed by Reuters, warned that such privacy tools make identity detection more difficult and flagged the app's use in cyber fraud.
Telegram lost a legal challenge last month against the temporary ban. (Reporting by Rajesh Kr. Singh, Munsif Vengattil, Aditya Kalra and Aftab Ahmed; Editing by Maju Samuel and Thomas Derpinghaus)
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Trump urges acting spy chief to declassify records, including on 2020 election
Article:
WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump said on Wednesday that his acting spy chief has wide permission to declassify records, including  any tied to the 2020 election, even though his close ally is only at the intelligence helm for a short time.
Trump appointed Bill Pulte as acting director of national intelligence last month, elevating a political loyalist with no national ​security experience at a time of war and global tensions.
Following a political backlash over his pick, Trump subsequently nominated Jay Clayton, the top US attorney for Manhattan.
But he then abruptly postponed Clayton's confirmation hearing in an effort to force Congress to pass a strict voter identification bill.
"Bill's there, just, you know, for maybe a month or two months or something," Trump told reporters as he departed Joint Base Andrews for an event in North Dakota.
"But while he's there, I said, 'You can declassify whatever you want'."
Asked if that included any records related to the 2020 election, Trump added: "I told him you could do it, it's fair. You got to ask him."
Trump, who won a second White House term in the 2024 election after losing in 2020, long has falsely claimed widespread fraud in US elections and continued to push debunked claims ahead of November's midterm contests as part of his pressure campaign to pass a strict voter identification law.
His comments come as NBC News reported that a White House task force was gathering documents related to the 2020 election with the aim of declassifying some of them.
"As the most transparent president in history, President Trump is totally committed to sharing as much information with the public as possible," said a White House official who requested anonymity.
The Office of the Director of National Intelligence and the office of Senate Intelligence Committee Chairman Tom Cotton did not respond to requests for comment on Trump's remarks.
The ODNI oversees the 18-agency US intelligence community that includes the premier foreign spy service, the Central Intelligence Agency, and the National Security Agency, the massive agency that eavesdrops on foreign communications and helps defend the United States against cyberattacks.
It was not clear when lawmakers would act on Clayton's nomination, but Trump told reporters there would be a hearing in two weeks.
A source familiar with the situation, on condition of anonymity, said Cotton, an Arkansas Republican, was "targeting" July 15.
Pulte currently leads the Federal Housing Finance Agency but will also remain as ODNI until Clayton is confirmed by the Senate. Pulte has reportedly already moved to cut hundreds of intelligence jobs.
Trump has said legislation known as the SAVE Act would deliver his fellow Republicans a "guaranteed" win in November as they seek to maintain their slim control of Congress.
Democrats are hoping to capitalise on Trump's low approval ratings to recapture at least the House of Representatives or the Senate.
Democrats and voting-rights advocates say the measure could hinder voter registration for millions of eligible Americans.
On Tuesday, Trump in a social media post said he had met with Tina Peters, a former county elections clerk in Colorado who was convicted of illegally tampering with voting machines in pursuit of Trump's false claims over the 2020 race.
Representatives of Peters did not immediately respond to a request for comment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid says PAS’ call to reject Pakatan will only matter if it translates into votes for BN on July 11
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today welcomed any additional support for Barisan Nasional (BN) after PAS urged voters to reject Pakatan Harapan (PH) in the Johor state election, but said the only measure that mattered would be the outcome at the ballot box on July 11.
Speaking to reporters after attending the 2025 Excellent Service Award Ceremony and Kejora Charity Donation at Lotus Desaru in Kota Tinggi, Zahid said BN respected the positions taken by other political coalitions, including PAS’ recent call for voters to withhold support from PH.
“That is their approach. We are campaigning professionally and concentrating on our candidates. Of course, if there is additional support, that is even better,” he said, as reported by
Utusan
Malaysia
.
“We want to see the outcome on July 11 when the ballot boxes are opened. That will be the real proof of whether what they are saying is true or otherwise.”
His remarks came after PAS president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang urged voters not to back PH in the Johor state election, including in constituencies where the contest is a straight fight between BN and PH.
He said the directive was among five resolutions adopted during a special meeting of the party’s Central Working Committee on Monday.
Asked about claims that the Johor election campaign had been subdued, Zahid dismissed the suggestion, saying BN’s campaign machinery remained active on the ground.
“The perception that the campaign is lacklustre may come from others, but BN believes the campaign is being carried out vigorously,” he said.
He added that every political coalition had the right to decide whether to support BN or otherwise.
“Naturally, we are grateful if that support is translated into votes on July 11 and helps secure victory for BN’s candidates in the Johor state election,” he said.
Johor voters will cast their ballots on July 11, with early voting set for July 7.

Title: As Pengerang’s population grows, DPM Zahid backs hospital and district status plans
Article:
KOTA TINGGI, July 2 — Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has agreed to a proposal to build a hospital in Pengerang, near here, citing its growing population.
He said the proposal would soon be brought to the Cabinet through the Housing and Local Government Ministry.
“It is very reasonable for a new hospital to cater and to address the problems of patients in the Pengerang area.
“I also support the application to turn Pengerang into a new district as proposed by the Pengerang MP (Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said) herself for approval,” he told reporters after the 2025 Excellent Service Award Ceremony and Kejora Charity Donation at Lotus Desaru here today.
Also present was Azalina, who is also minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform), in her capacity as the Pengerang MP.
Ahmad Zahid, who is also the rural and regional development minister, said the construction of a hospital and turning Pengerang into a new district were part of Kejora’s long-term plans in collaboration with the Johor state government.
Earlier in his speech, Ahmad Zahid said the presentation of Kejora’s annual report at yesterday’s Cabinet meeting showed encouraging achievements.
He said the organisation, which comes under the Rural and Regional Development Ministry, had recorded good performance and expressed hope that it would continue.
Kejora was founded on June 1, 1972, as a statutory body responsible for driving socio-economic development, housing, infrastructure, agriculture and tourism across the southeastern region of Johor. It originally covered 16 per cent of Johor, mainly spanning the Kota Tinggi and Kluang districts.

Title: DAP bets on new generation of candidates to shake up Johor election
Article:
BATU PAHAT, July 2 — DAP is confident that its line-up of first-time candidates contesting under the Pakatan Harapan (PH) banner in the Johor state election can bring meaningful change to their respective constituencies.
DAP deputy secretary-general Steven Sim Chee Keong said the Johor DAP leadership’s decision to field candidates who had never contested an election reflected the party’s commitment to giving younger leaders the opportunity to step forward and serve the people.
Sim, who is also the Minister of Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives said although they were new faces in electoral politics, the candidates were not newcomers to the political arena, as many had gained extensive experience working behind the scenes for the party.
“We are giving them the opportunity to serve as candidates and, if entrusted by the people, they will go on to become elected representatives. While we are introducing young and new candidates, we are not sidelining our senior leaders.
“We will continue to involve those who have previously served as elected representatives and party leaders in our campaign and grassroots efforts,” he told Bernama after a breakfast session with DAP’s Parit Raja state seat candidate here today.
Also present were DAP assistant national publicity secretary Young Syefura Othman and the party’s Parit Raja candidate Shazwan Dzainal Abidin.
Young Syefura said the party was fully backing its new candidates to demonstrate that every individual within DAP possessed the capability to lead, while also providing them with the opportunity to showcase their potential at the grassroots level.
She said Shazwan, who hails from Batu Pahat, has nearly a decade of political experience, including serving as special officer to the Penggaram state assemblyman.
Young Syefura also praised Shazwan’s positive spirit throughout the first six days of campaigning, despite Parit Raja being widely regarded as one of Barisan Nasional’s strongholds.
Meanwhile, Shazwan said he was encouraged by the warm reception from local residents, admitting that he had initially felt nervous about contesting in the constituency.
“I am excited to be contesting in what has traditionally been an UMNO and BN stronghold. However, the response from the people has been very encouraging. Some even approached me for selfies, and that has given me added confidence,” he said.
In the Johor polls, DAP, contesting under the PH banner, is fielding 17 candidates in Jementah, Bekok, Tangkak, Bentayan, Yong Peng, Parit Raja, Penggaram, Mengkibol, Paloh, Tiram, Johor Jaya, Stulang, Perling, Skudai, Bukit Permai, Senai and Pekan Nanas.
A total of 172 candidates are contesting in the Johor state election, with polling on July 11 and early voting on July 7. — Bernama

Title: Seven in 10 Klang Valley adults overweight or obese, raising risks of heart, kidney and diabetes
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Seven in 10 adults screened in the Klang Valley were found to be overweight or obese, underscoring Malaysia’s growing cardio renal metabolic (CRM) health crisis, according to the National Cancer Society Malaysia (NCSM) and Boehringer Ingelheim.
Findings from two policy briefs, supported by the Health Ministry, revealed that 2,065 of 5,000 individuals screened under the Saring@Komuniti programme were living with obesity. Overall, 41.3 per cent were obese and 28.8 per cent overweight, while 34.5 per cent had pre diabetes and 35.1 per cent diabetes.
The reports showed that 97.8 per cent of participants had at least one CRM risk factor, with nearly 68 per cent facing three or more. Obesity was the most common condition, followed by diabetes and hyperlipidaemia.
NCSM managing director Professor Dr Murallitharan Munisamy said the findings highlight the need to treat cardiovascular, kidney and metabolic health as a connected continuum.
“Malaysia has an opportunity to shift from managing individual diseases separately to addressing CRM health holistically. Early detection must be matched by coordinated follow up and long term care if we are to improve outcomes and reduce the growing burden of chronic disease,” he said in a statement today.
The briefs also noted that rural and underserved communities face significant barriers in accessing healthcare and co screening programmes for CRM syndrome.

Title: Amirudin: Selangor economy surges to RM460.1b in 2025, contributing 26.5pc of Malaysia’s GDP
Article:
SHAH ALAM, July 2 — Selangor’s economy expanded by RM28 billion in 2025 to reach RM460.1 billion, accounting for 26.5 per cent of Malaysia’s gross domestic product (GDP), Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said today.
He said the growth, which exceeded projections by Universiti Putra Malaysia and the Selangor Research Institute, was more than double that of any other state. Selangor’s economy is now 1.7 times the size of Kuala Lumpur’s and 2.7 times Johor’s.
The state recorded a growth rate of 6.3 per cent last year, surpassing the national average of 5.2 per cent. The expansion was driven by the services sector (RM15.9 billion), manufacturing (RM5.3 billion) and construction (RM3.7 billion).
Selangor now contributes 35.9 per cent of Malaysia’s construction activity, 32.8 per cent of manufacturing and 27.1 per cent of services.
Amirudin said the achievement was made possible through the First Selangor Plan (RS‑1), a five‑year socio‑economic blueprint from 2021 to 2025, which saw the state economy grow by 33.9 per cent, or RM116.6 billion.
He added that the achievement was not solely the result of policymaking in Shah Alam, but also the collective efforts of the people of Selangor.
Looking ahead, Amirudin said the state must set its sights on becoming the first in Malaysia to reach a RM500 billion economy, a goal that will require close collaboration between the public service, the workforce and the private sector.
He emphasised that future policies will prioritise improving living standards, ensuring that economic progress translates into tangible benefits for the people.
“I would like to thank the civil service, industries investing in the state, and above all the people of Selangor for their dedication and contribution to this economic success,” he said.

Title: Saifuddin Nasution says MyNIISe app hits 2.4 million downloads, 19.48 million QR scans easing Johor Causeway jams
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, July 2 — The Home Ministry’s MyNIISe application has recorded more than 19.48 million QR transactions at the Sultan Iskandar Building (BSI) and Sultan Abu Bakar Complex (KSAB, Johor) as of June 28, 2026, said Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail.
He said the app, which facilitates faster immigration clearance, has been downloaded 2.4 million times, with 1.27 million registered users.
“The growing use of digital lanes has eased congestion at the country’s two main entry points,” he said in a statement.
Saifuddin added that the initiative has also been extended to five major airports nationwide, where 5.59 million transactions have been recorded.
He said the MyNIISe rollout reflects the Madani Government’s commitment to performance and delivery, ensuring reforms translate into more efficient services that directly benefit the public.
“Digital transformations such as MyNIISe will continue to be strengthened to improve service delivery, ease daily affairs and enhance the efficiency of Malaysia’s immigration system,” he said.

Title: Government to kick off 18‑month National Gentleman Study to see if men can be wise, kind and tidy
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, July 2 — The Ministry of Women, Family and Community Development (KPWKM) will kick off the
Kajian Lelaki Budiman Negara
(National Gentleman Study) this month under the 13th Malaysia Plan (13MP) to develop evidence-based policies and initiatives for men’s empowerment.
Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri said the 18-month study would support the government’s National Gentleman Initiative, aimed at nurturing resilient, responsible men who can contribute meaningfully to family well-being and national development.
She said the government recognises that men’s empowerment should be viewed through a broader lens, extending beyond leadership and economic participation to include emotional resilience, mental well-being, maturity and the ability to shoulder responsibilities with integrity.
“At the same time, men’s empowerment must be grounded in the principle of gender respect, fostering a culture of mutual respect, appreciation and dignity for every individual.
“A gentleman is not someone who dominates, but one who leads with wisdom, shares responsibilities and respects women as equal partners in building strong families,” she said when opening the Men’s Empowerment Consultative Forum here today.
